hero.name: I'm Suracha Hanthongchai (Pom Pam)
hero.subtitle: I build scalable applications and healthcare software, and I weave AI into real-world workflows — from hospital systems to the classroom.

about.heading: About Me
about.bio1: I'm a software developer and computer educator based in Thailand. My core work is healthcare software — designing and maintaining EHP hospital modules that clinicians rely on every day — alongside full-stack development with React, Go, and SQL.
about.bio2: Outside production code, I teach computer science, coach student robotics teams, and speak about practical AI adoption. I believe great software starts with understanding real users, and the best classrooms are the ones where students ship real projects.
about.quote: You say it can't be done — have you tried?

kku.imageAlt: Graduation portrait in Khon Kaen University gown

kamphaeng.imageAlt: Student portrait in Kamphaeng School uniform

ehp.title: Healthcare EHP Modules
ehp.description: Modules for a hospital information system (EHP) that clinical staff use in real Thai hospitals every day — such as the patient home-visit registry shown (patient data blurred). Built with Delphi, Go, and SQL across clinical and back-office workflows; deeper details are withheld under the company's confidentiality agreement.

metaherb.title: Metaherb E-commerce Platform
metaherb.description: A production e-commerce platform for a Thai herbal brand, live at metaherb.co.th — storefront with categories and reviews, cart and PromptPay QR checkout, coupons, order management, and an admin analytics dashboard with a built-in AI product assistant. Designed and built entirely solo with React + Go + Docker through a fully AI-assisted, spec-driven workflow.

roblox.title: Learn-to-Code Roblox Game
roblox.description: "Game Rian Code" — a published Roblox world that teaches coding logic through quests and puzzles, built with Roblox Studio and Luau and used in real classroom activities. Playable on Roblox.

jo.title: Fun Math Game (Year-1 Project)
jo.description: A forest-adventure desktop math game built in my first year at university: pick +, −, × or ÷ mode, then pair number bubbles to hit the target answer against the clock. C# WinForms with Guna UI, plus sign-up/login and per-player score history stored in MySQL.

contact.heading: Get in Touch
contact.intro: Have a project, a class, or an idea to talk about? Reach me through whichever channel is easiest — I usually reply within a day or two.
